root_text.sn46.27:1.1: “Yo, bhikkhave, maggo yā paṭipadā taṇhānirodhāya saṁvattati, taṁ maggaṁ taṁ paṭipadaṁ bhāvetha.
root_text.sn46.27:1.2: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, maggo katamā ca paṭipadā taṇhānirodhāya saṁvattati?
root_text.sn46.27:1.7: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, satta bojjhaṅgā kathaṁ bahulīkatā taṇhānirodhāya saṁvattanti?
root_text.sn46.27:2.1: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.27:2.2: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.27:2.3: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, satta bojjhaṅgā evaṁ bahulīkatā taṇhānirodhāya saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.27:1.1: “Mendicants, you should develop the path and the practice that leads to the cessation of craving.
translation_text.sn46.27:1.2: And what is the path and the practice that leads to the cessation of craving?
translation_text.sn46.27:1.4: It is the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.27:1.6: The awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.27:1.7: And how are the seven awakening factors developed and cultivated so as to lead to the cessation of craving?
translation_text.sn46.27:2.1: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.27:2.2: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.27:2.3: This is how the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated so as to lead to the cessation of craving.”
